title: Quick question PWM
wikitext:
[]: Quick question: PWM             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:53 pm This has been bothering me for the last 45 minutes: what exactly is Particle Wave Matter (PWM)? I've read mentions of this term on various threads, yet I do not believe that I have an accurate definition. The current definition in my mind is somewhere along the lines of "organic matter", am I close? Looked around on the commentary geektionary and Google to no avail, nor did I succeed in sifting through past posts. Help me out! :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 23, 2007 8:19 pm It is the stuff from which uber-beings are made. Listen to Ritsuko babbling about the Shamshel research findings in #05.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Mon Apr 23, 2007 8:28 pm I'm guessing it would be a kind of matter where "particle" and "wave" are inadequate descriptions. I've been a little confused by this myself, doesn't quantum mechanics state that all matter demonstrates the particle/wave duality?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Apr 24, 2007 12:53 pm Ayana-Me wrote: doesn't quantum mechanics state that all matter demonstrates the particle/wave duality?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Apr 24, 2007 10:01 pm Ok, I took some physics back in the day: Basically, everything in the universe is either "matter" or "energy" So you've got "light" which is actually "electro-magnetic radiation". "Electro-magnetic radiation" is made of discrete particles called "photons" (this is what Einstein won the Nobel Prize for when he proved this) Matter is made of Protons, Neutrons, and Electrons. Protons and Neutrons are made of Quarks. Electrons are a form of Lepton. (There are other types of Quarks and Leptons, but they don't exist naturally on Earth) ****At any rate, ORIGINALLY they thought that "electro-magnetic energy" acts as a "wave", and it does, but it does have some "particle" aspects to it because it exists as Photons. Meanwhile, while studying electrons, they found out that while they are "matter particles" they have some wave-like properties. This is the Uncertianty Principle: it is impossible to know where they are and what direction they are travelling in at the same time. And given further research, they found out that even Protons and Neutrons have some wave-like properties, it's just that they're so heavy that such wave properties are negligible. So it's actually a spectrum from: Protons--->Electrons------------------------------->Photons Protons have NEGLIGIBLE wave properties, Electrons are mostly particle but have SOME noticeable wave properties, and Photons are almost entirely wave but have some particle properties. ****So the idea is, some have speculated that there might be "stuff" which is between Electrons and Protons, which isn't entirely wavelike and not entirely particle-like.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Apr 24, 2007 10:23 pm V wrote: ****So the idea is, some have speculated that there might be "stuff" which is between Electrons and Protons, which isn't entirely wavelike and not entirely particle-like.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Wed Apr 25, 2007 7:49 am maybe like how light is supposed to be...

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Apr 25, 2007 7:59 am string theory is beyond that; that explains how quarks and leptons are made and such. I think the episode just said "it's both a particle and a wave"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 25, 2007 9:30 am Episode #05 (Plat) wrote: RITSUKO: We do know the Angel is composed of a type of matter characterized by both particulate and wave properties, like light.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Apr 26, 2007 1:24 pm V wrote: string theory is beyond that; that explains how quarks and leptons are made and such. I think the episode just said "it's both a particle and a wave"

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Apr 26, 2007 4:37 pm V wrote: So it's actually a spectrum from: Protons--->Electrons------------------------------->Photons Protons have NEGLIGIBLE wave properties, Electrons are mostly particle but have SOME noticeable wave properties, and Photons are almost entirely wave but have some particle properties. ****So the idea is, some have speculated that there might be "stuff" which is between Electrons and Protons, which isn't entirely wavelike and not entirely particle-like.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Apr 26, 2007 6:45 pm I think everyone is looking at this slightly the wrong way. On a purely practicle level our bodies are constructed of cells, the Angels on the other hand are made solely of this odd form of matter that allows them to shape-shift and other really cool stuff. Their equivilent of DNA is made of this stuff, they are purely particulate/waveform matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 26, 2007 7:35 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Their equivilent of DNA is made of this stuff, they are purely particulate/waveform matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Apr 26, 2007 8:14 pm Kinda hard to say. I'm inclined to think it is DNA, just not in the terms we usually think of it as due to their nature.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Apr 26, 2007 9:08 pm Didn't Ritsuko say something about Angel DNA being very close to human, like a 98% similarity? Meaning, they actually have DNA. Or did I just hallucinate that?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 26, 2007 9:08 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Kinda hard to say. I'm inclined to think it is DNA, just not in the terms we usually think of it as due to their nature.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Apr 26, 2007 9:12 pm Chromatin for the win.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Apr 26, 2007 9:33 pm BrikHaus wrote: Didn't Ritsuko say something about Angel DNA being very close to human, like a 98% similarity? Meaning, they actually have DNA. Or did I just hallucinate that?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Apr 27, 2007 11:34 pm Reichu wrote: Well, you know, either they have this stringy crap floating around inside cell nuclei or they don't...

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Sat May 05, 2007 12:22 pm Reichu wrote: It is the stuff from which uber-beings are made. Listen to Ritsuko babbling about the Shamshel research findings in #05.
